bottom.markdown:
## Join the movement to restore our forests

Every tree planted creates a lasting impact for communities and biodiversity.

info.markdown:
# About Future Forest

We restore degraded forests and create new sustainable forests...

# Our Impact

Trees planted, biodiversity restored, communities supported.

hero.title: Rewilding the UK's landscapes at scale

socialProof.titleTemplate: Join {count}+ supporters to restore ecosystems, protect wildlife and combat climate change.

projects.heading: Our 11 large-scale projects across India and Cambodia
projects.description: We've planted over 1.5 million predominantly native trees and restored 1500 hectares of meadows, wetland and peatland.

supportCovers.heading: What your support covers
supportCovers.description: Restoring nature is much more than just planting. Your support covers all the steps from seedlings to watering.

items.title: Step 1: Make seedlings
items.description: Start by preparing seedlings in a controlled environment

items.title: Step 2: Prep the soil
items.description: Prepare nutrient-rich soil for optimal growth

items.title: Step 3: Plant & Water
items.description: Transfer seedlings and maintain proper hydration

items.title: Step 4: Mulch & Protect
items.description: Apply organic mulch to retain moisture and prevent weeds

items.title: Step 5: Monitor Growth
items.description: Track plant health and adjust care as needed

items.title: Step 6: Prune & Train
items.description: Shape plants for optimal growth and structure

items.title: Step 7: Support Wildlife
items.description: Create habitats for beneficial insects and animals

items.title: Step 8: Harvest & Document
items.description: Collect seeds and record restoration progress

items.title: Step 9: Expand & Repeat
items.description: Scale the restoration to new areas using proven methods

supporters.description: Every day people join the community of {partnerName}. Together we've restored 1200m² of nature:

wildlife.heading: Wildlife returning to our projects
wildlife.description: See the incredible biodiversity returning to our restored habitats as documented by our field team.

impactSection.heading: Nature is disappearing at a devastating rate in the UK, but with your help, we can bring life back.
impactSection.description: By restoring meadows, wetlands, and forests you're not simply putting plants in the ground, you're rebuilding ecosystems that:
benefits: Lock away carbon for generations to come and help climate change.
Support wildlife and biodiversity by providing essential habitats
Support clean water, fertile soils, and resilient landscapes.
impactSection.milestonesDescription: Here's what's happening at the 4 locations Justin helped restore, straight from our team on the ground.
milestones: Planted over 1.5 million predominantly native trees
Restored 1500 hectares of meadows, wetland and peatland
These 10 species have been positively impacted by the work we've done
Engaged communities to experience nature up close
impactSection.additionalHeading: Wetlands and meadows are absolutely essential
impactSection.additionalDescription: They provide a diverse habitat for animals and rare plants, and they support the pollinators we depend on. They prevent flooding, and strengthen our resilience to drought. They store huge amounts of carbon, and even improve our water quality. You can read a lot more about meadows and peatland on our website.
